metadata.name: pconn
metadata.summary: Platform Connectors that can interface different platforms to provide specific services.

documentation: Setup an interface for Gallagher plugins.

Process all dependencies and requirements for a module.

documentation:
Ensure all dependencies are set up.

Returns a list of dependencies which failed to set up.

functions.name: pconn.plugin_entry_flow._map_error_to_schema_errors
documentation:
Map an error to the correct position in the schema_errors.

Raises ValueError if the error path could not be found in the schema.
Limitation: Nested schemas are not supported and a ValueError will be raised.

Create a flow for specified handler.

Manage all the flows that are in progress.

functions.name: pconn.util.package.is_virtual_env
documentation: Return if we run in a virtual environment.

functions.name: pconn.util.package.is_installed
documentation:
Check if a package is installed and will be loaded when we import it.

expected input is a pip compatible package specifier (requirement string)
e.g. "package==1.0.0" or "package>=1.0.0,<2.0.0"

For backward compatibility, it also accepts a URL with a fragment
e.g. "git+https://github.com/pypa/pip#pip>=1"

Returns True when the requirement is met.
Returns False when the package is not installed or doesn't meet req.

functions.name: pconn.util.package.install_package
documentation:
Install a package on PyPi. Accepts pip compatible package strings.

Return boolean if install successful.

functions.name: pconn.requirements.async_process_requirements
documentation:
Install the requirements for a component or platform.

This method is a coroutine. It will raise RequirementsNotFound
if an requirement can't be satisfied.

functions.name: pconn.requirements._install_with_retry
documentation: Try to install a package up to MAX_INSTALL_FAILURES times.

methods.name: pconn.requirements.RequirementsManager.async_process_requirements

methods.name: pconn.requirements.RequirementsManager._find_missing_requirements
documentation: Find requirements that are missing in the cache.

methods.name: pconn.requirements.RequirementsManager._raise_for_failed_requirements

Raise RequirementsNotFound so we do not keep trying requirements
that have already failed.

methods.name: pconn.core.PConn.async_verify_app_state

methods.name: pconn.core.PConn.async_create_task
documentation:
Create a task from within the event loop.

This method must be run in the event loop. If you are using this in your
integration, use the create task methods on the config entry instead.

target: target to call.

methods.name: pconn.core.PConn.async_create_background_task
documentation:
Create a task from within the event loop.

This is a background task which will not block startup and will be
automatically cancelled on shutdown. If you are using this in your
integration, use the create task methods on the config entry instead.

This method must be run in the event loop.

methods.name: pconn.core.PConn.async_stop
documentation: Stop Gallagher Plugins and all plugin entries.

functions.name: pconn.util.ulid.ulid
documentation:
Generate a ULID.

This ulid should not be used for cryptographically secure
operations.

 01AN4Z07BY      79KA1307SR9X4MV3
|----------|    |----------------|
 Timestamp          Randomness
   48bits             80bits

This string can be loaded directly with https://github.com/ahawker/ulid

import homeassistant.util.ulid as ulid_util
import ulid
ulid.parse(ulid_util.ulid())

functions.name: pconn.util.logger.async_activate_log_queue_handler

Migrate the existing log handlers to use the queue.

This allows us to avoid blocking I/O and formatting messages
in the event loop as log messages are written in another thread.

methods.name: pconn.util.logger.LogErrorHandler.emit
documentation:
Save error and warning logs.

Everything logged with error or warning is saved in local buffer. A
default upper limit is set to 50 (older entries are discarded) but can
be changed if needed.

documentation: Add a function to call when config entry is unloaded.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntry._async_process_on_unload
documentation: Process the on_unload callbacks and wait for pending tasks.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntry.async_get_view
documentation: Call the action and return the result.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntry.async_create_task
documentation:
Create a task from within the event loop.

This method must be run in the event loop.

target: target to call.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntry.async_create_background_task
documentation:
Create a background task tied to the config entry lifecycle.

Background tasks are automatically canceled when config entry is unloaded.

target: target to call.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow.__init_subclass__
documentation: Initialize a subclass, register if possible.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow.async_get_options_flow
documentation: Get the options flow for this handler.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow.async_supports_options_flow
documentation: Return options flow support for this handler.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow._async_abort_entries_match
documentation:
Abort if current entries match all data.

Requires `already_configured` in strings.json in user visible flows.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow._abort_if_ws_id_configured
documentation: Abort if the plugin is already configured for this workstation.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow.limit_reached
documentation: Return true if the license limit has been reached.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntryFlow.async_step_user
documentation: Handle a flow initiated by the user.

classes.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginOptionsFlow
documentation: Base class for options flows with plugin entry and options.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_domains
documentation: Return plugin domains which have entries.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_entries
documentation: Return all entries or entries for a specific domain.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_entries_by_domain
documentation: Return all entries grouped by domain.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_setup
documentation:
Set up a config entry.

Return True if entry has been successfully loaded.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_reload
documentation:
Reload an entry.

If an entry was not loaded, will just load.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_set_disabled_by
documentation:
Disable an entry.

If disabled_by is changed, the config entry will be reloaded.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_get_view
documentation: Call the action and return the result.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.get_event_stream
documentation: Call the action and return the result.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntires.async_get_platform_entry
documentation: Return a plugin entry for a platform.

documentation: Creates a plugin flow for the given domain.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.PluginEntriesFlowManager.async_finish_flow
documentation: Finish a config flow and add an entry.

classes.name: pconn.plugin_entries.OptionsFlowManager

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.OptionsFlowManager._async_get_plugin_entry
documentation: Return plugin entry or raise if not found.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.OptionsFlowManager.async_create_flow
documentation:
Create an options flow for a plugin entry.

Entry_id and flow.handler is the same thing to map entry with flow.

methods.name: pconn.plugin_entries.OptionsFlowManager.async_finish_flow
documentation:
Finish an options flow and update options for plugin entry.

Flow.handler and entry_id is the same thing to map flow with entry.
Flow to set options for a configuration entry.
